title: The One In Vegas

jokes.text: Phoebe, could you get that, please? -Why? Just because you're too lazy to get up off your tushy?
jokes.setup: Simple request to answer phone
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Phoebe's directness, but fairly predictable exchange

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Can you answer the phone?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Why? So you can avoid getting up off the couch?

jokes.text: No, it's just that all the people in the world I would want to talk to are right here.
jokes.setup: Expected defensive response about laziness
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly sweet sentiment that reframes the situation
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted misdirection that reveals character sweetness while defusing conflict

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm not lazy! I just... I like to think of myself as 'energy efficient.'

jokes.text: Sucker.
jokes.setup: Monica's sweet sentiment about friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing, very Phoebe to puncture sweet moments

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, you're all gonna die soon and I'm gonna eat all your stuff.

jokes.text: I scratched it on the ATM machine on the corner.
jokes.setup: Joey needs help remembering PIN
jokes.punchline: He literally scratched it on the ATM for everyone to see
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - solves one problem by creating a much bigger one

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can't remember my PIN!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So what did you do?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I scratched it into the ATM so I wouldn't forget it again.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey! That's your pin number! Anyone can see it now!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No they can't, I wrote it really small.

jokes.text: My stunt double. Yeah. He's getting a little too familiar for my tastes.
jokes.setup: Authoritative voice telling Joey to get back to work
jokes.punchline: It's his stunt double acting superior
jokes.explanation: Great reversal of expectations - the employee is giving orders to the star

dialogue.speaker: Stunt Double
dialogue.line: Get back to work.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Who do you think you are?

dialogue.speaker: Stunt Double
dialogue.line: I'm your stunt double.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, you can't talk to me like that!

dialogue.speaker: Stunt Double
dialogue.line: Actually, I can. I'm doubling for you.

jokes.text: What was that? -Entertainment Tonight.
jokes.setup: People asking for pictures with Joey
jokes.punchline: It's Entertainment Tonight, implying he's actually famous
jokes.explanation: Nice twist showing Joey might actually have made it

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So what, random people just come up to you asking for pictures?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, it happens all the time now.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really? Where?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It was Entertainment Tonight.

jokes.text: Which Richard? -The Richard. Richard Simmons? Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Monica mentions running into Richard
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection playing on celebrity name recognition

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, you ran into Richard? Richard Simmons?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, not Richard Simmons. Richard Burke. My ex-boyfriend.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you ran into him? Where? What did he say?

jokes.text: Okay, that actually makes more sense.
jokes.setup: Learning it's Monica's ex-boyfriend Richard, not Richard Simmons
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that sells the absurdity of the Richard Simmons assumption

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, it's Richard Burke, not Richard Simmons!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, that makes so much more sense.

jokes.text: That's weird. I dreamed you had lunch with Richard. -Really? -Again, Richard Simmons.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentions dreaming about Monica and Richard
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that maintains the running gag without overdoing it

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I had this dream about Monica and Richard.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Richard? My Richard?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, I'm still thinking about Richard Simmons.

jokes.text: Wow, I owe Rachel 20 bucks. On a totally different bet.
jokes.setup: Rachel owes money about Monica and Chandler lasting
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment suggesting inappropriate betting between friends

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So you guys are actually staying together?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes, Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh thank God, because I have twenty bucks riding on you two breaking up.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean... not because I want you to break up. I just... I bet Joey that you wouldn't last.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: How long?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: A month.

jokes.text: Now you're just my annoying friend Chandler.
jokes.setup: Chandler's romantic sentiment about their anniversary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica response - loving but brutally honest about Chandler's annoying qualities

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Happy anniversary. I got you something special.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you didn't have to do that! But since you did, I hope you kept the receipt.

jokes.text: We can call it our 'Plane-aversary.'
jokes.setup: Celebrating anniversary on the plane
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler wordplay - forced but in character

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're celebrating our anniversary on a plane. It's very romantic. We're in the air, we're in love... we're... we're in-love!

jokes.text: Do we have to?
jokes.setup: Chandler's 'Plane-aversary' suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response to Chandler's wordplay

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No. Absolutely not. We are not calling it that.

jokes.text: Plus, we get to have our own 'Anna-Vegas-ry'! 'Anna-vada-versary!'
jokes.setup: Already established Chandler's tendency for bad portmanteaus
jokes.explanation: Escalating the bad wordplay trend, getting progressively worse

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Vegas? More like... Vegass. With an extra S. For... super.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Or Veg-ASS. You know, 'cause it's in the desert.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Veg-NASTY? No wait—Veg-ASS-ity.

jokes.text: I think we should see other people.
jokes.setup: Chandler's increasingly bad anniversary puns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - perfectly timed nuclear response to bad puns. Great Monica moment.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I got you a present for our anniversary. I hope it's not a letdown... unlike our relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, if you make one more pun, I'm going to kill you in your sleep.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wow, that's a bit of an overreaction. I was just trying to have some fun... anniversary fun, get it?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's it. I'm done. I'm taking the apartment, the furniture, the dog we don't have, and I'm moving to Yemen.

jokes.text: You won't ditch me like you did with London. Ditch you? You were pregnant
jokes.setup: Phoebe accusing Monica of ditching her
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established show history with Phoebe's selective memory

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You ditched me!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I didn't ditch you. I had to work late.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's what ditchers say.

jokes.text: Art lover.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentioning Van Gogh exhibit
jokes.punchline: Ross's dismissive comment delivered as insult
jokes.explanation: Good Ross moment showing his intellectual snobbishness and petty side

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Van Gogh? Please. The man cut off his own ear. I mean, what kind of artist does that?

jokes.text: Was that supposed to be an insult? -I don't know, I'm very tired.
jokes.setup: Rachel questioning Ross's weak insult attempt
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross's awkwardness when his intellectual superiority attempts fail

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What was that supposed to mean?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Nothing, I just... you know what, never mind.

jokes.text: To walk around naked. No, so I can be by myself. Have a little alone time. Naked alone time.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming he wants apartment to himself for solitude
jokes.explanation: Good character reveal showing Ross's inability to maintain pretense

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I just want some time alone, you know? For solitude and quiet reflection.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Plus I can walk around naked.

jokes.text: Why does it takes me so long to answer the door?
jokes.setup: Ross claiming he doesn't walk around naked when alone
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-incriminating moment with perfect logic and timing

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't just walk around naked when I'm alone.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, you do.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, I don't!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Dude, I've seen you.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, that doesn't... I mean, that was one time!

jokes.text: Las Vegas, 1. London, 0.
jokes.setup: Phoebe getting candy on plane
jokes.explanation: Establishes Phoebe's competitive running gag about comparing trips

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I got candy on the plane! This is so exciting! I'm keeping score between trips now.

jokes.text: Happy Plane-aversary.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment with Monica on plane
jokes.punchline: Using the terrible pun Monica rejected earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Chandler can't help himself with the bad wordplay

jokes.text: How about 'I did forget the present, forgive me' not fake-out?
jokes.setup: Chandler's fake-out about forgetting present
jokes.explanation: Great reversal - turning his joke setup into her actual confession

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I actually forgot your birthday present!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? You forgot?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm sorry. I got you something, but then I realized it was stupid, so I returned it. And then I forgot to get you something else.

jokes.text: Simmons. Go with Simmons.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking about Richard thing
jokes.punchline: Rachel desperately trying to redirect to Richard Simmons
jokes.explanation: Good panic moment bringing back the Richard Simmons misdirection

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So what's the deal with you and Richard?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh! Richard Simmons! Have you seen him lately? He's got amazing energy!

jokes.text: Okay, London, 1. This is gonna be a real horserace.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler's fight on plane
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the running gag with sports commentary tone

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, so Monica and Chandler had a fight on the plane, which means... Joey and I are still tied.

jokes.text: Check me out. I'm in my kitchen... naked. I'm picking up an orange. I'm naked.
jokes.setup: Ross alone in apartment wanting to be naked
jokes.punchline: Narrating his naked activities like a nature documentary
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Ross's characteristic over-narration

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And here we see the male of the species in his natural habitat, completely unclothed and vulnerable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: He approaches the refrigerator, searching for sustenance, unaware that he is being observed.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Notice how he carefully adjusts the thermostat, ensuring optimal comfort for his exposed skin.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! That's Rachel, naked!
jokes.setup: Ross enjoying being naked alone
jokes.punchline: Suddenly seeing Rachel naked across the way
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the naked situation into unexpected voyeurism

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, this is nice. Just me, completely naked, no one around...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait... is that... Rachel?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

jokes.text: Unless she wants me to be looking at that. She knows I'm home. She knows I can see her. What kind of game is she playing?
jokes.setup: Ross seeing Rachel naked accidentally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross neuroses - turning accident into elaborate conspiracy theory

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: She wanted me to see her. She definitely wanted me to see her. Why else would she be naked? She was sending me a signal. A very clear, very naked signal.

jokes.text: The physical act of love.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to explain what their encounter would be about
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross - making sex sound like a scientific procedure. Perfect character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, it would be a fascinating encounter from a paleontological perspective. We could discuss the evolutionary advantages of bipedalism, perhaps examine some fossilized remains, and engage in a rigorous academic dialogue about Mesozoic extinction events.

jokes.text: Oh, wow. I'm sorry. But, Ross, you kicked off your shoes.
jokes.setup: Ross's elaborate seduction misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Evidence was just him removing shoes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of Ross's fantasy with mundane reality. Great writing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, so all that 'evidence' of him trying to seduce me was just... him removing his shoes?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I was getting comfortable!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You literally spent twenty minutes explaining how taking off your footwear was a classic seduction technique.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, it's in the Paleontology Manual of Dating!

jokes.text: For ninety-nine cents I'd eat you.
jokes.setup: Phoebe being reminded she doesn't eat animals
jokes.punchline: Would eat people for cheap enough price
jokes.explanation: Dark Phoebe humor with perfect economy - cannibalism for value prices

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, if the price was right, I'd eat people.

jokes.text: Love your condoms, my man.
jokes.setup: Joey in gladiator costume
jokes.explanation: Good visual payoff showing how ridiculous Joey's costume looks in public

jokes.text: Monica had lunch with Richard. -Dawson?
jokes.setup: Mention of Richard
jokes.punchline: Joey thinking of game show host
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment showing his pop culture brain over relationship awareness

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, Richard? He's so sophisticated and mature.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, he sounds like a game show host.

jokes.text: Now, I'm just letting you know... this is not an invitation to the physical act of love.
jokes.setup: Rachel removing sweater
jokes.explanation: Good callback mocking Ross's ridiculous terminology

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm sorry, did my hair get in the way of your career?

jokes.text: But it was a first for the rest of my building.
jokes.setup: Discussion about embarrassment from naked incident
jokes.punchline: Revealing the whole building saw Rachel naked
jokes.explanation: Great escalation revealing the scope of Rachel's exposure

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's not that bad. I mean, so what if a few people saw you naked?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: A few people?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. The whole building saw you naked.

jokes.text: Hey, lady! I don't care how much you want it, okay? I am not gonna have sex with you in the bathroom!
jokes.setup: Ross claiming he doesn't embarrass easily
jokes.punchline: Rachel loudly embarrassing him in casino
jokes.explanation: Brilliant public humiliation perfectly timed to prove Ross wrong about not being embarrassed

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't embarrass easily.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh really? ROSS! ROSS! Everyone, this is my friend Ross. He's never been with a woman!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel!

jokes.text: If I won $5000, I'd join a gym. Build up my upper body... and hit Richard from behind with a stick.
jokes.setup: Chandler's plan if he won money
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Chandler's passive-aggressive fantasy turned violent

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: If I won the lottery, I'd have an elaborate scheme to assault Richard.

jokes.text: Yeah! Las Vegas! Number one!
jokes.setup: Monica going to fix relationship with Chandler
jokes.explanation: Continuing the running gag with Phoebe's competitive spirit

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, we're going to Vegas! We're going to Vegas! We're going to Vegas!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm gonna wear a low-cut top and have a fake ID that says I'm a cocktail waitress!

jokes.text: My identical hand twin!
jokes.setup: Joey excited about discovery
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey absurdity - completely nonsensical concept delivered with total conviction

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. I just realized something. We could be hand twins!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hand twins?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah! You know, like identical twins, but just for hands. We could go on talk shows and be like, 'Look at our identical hands!'

jokes.text: You sure you weren't looking at your hands in a mirror?
jokes.setup: Joey's excitement about identical hands
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deflation of Joey's absurd excitement with simple logic

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Look at this! Our hands are identical!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, we're not identical twins. You have your hands, I have mine. They just... look like hands.

jokes.text: How much would you pay to see this hand... twice?
jokes.setup: Joey explaining his business model
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous sales pitch for seeing identical hands
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey logic - treating mundane body part like premium entertainment

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, so here's the thing. I've got this amazing business opportunity. You pay me fifty bucks, and I show you my hands. Then I show you my hands again. Identical! Same hands! How'd I do it?

jokes.text: Oh, you're so lucky!
jokes.setup: Joey explaining his identical hand twin discovery
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sincere enthusiasm for absurd concept
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe moment - her willingness to believe in Joey's nonsense

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's amazing! You have a hand twin? That's like... that's like finding your soulmate, but for hands!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You guys, this is so romantic. I'm getting emotional.

jokes.text: And you're Bob Saget.
jokes.setup: Phoebe guessing Ross's mustache makes him look like Pancho Villa
jokes.punchline: Guessing Rachel looks like TV host
jokes.explanation: Random Phoebe pop culture reference with visual callback to drawn-on faces

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You look like Pancho Villa!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I do not look like Pancho Villa.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, you do a little bit.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And Rachel looks like Vanna White!

jokes.text: Dance karate? Yes, it's a deadly... but beautiful sport.
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentioning dance karate lessons
jokes.punchline: Her poetic description of nonsensical sport
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe absurdity - making up activities that sound plausible to her

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm taking dance karate. It's like a beautiful blend of movement and self-defense. You leap through the air, and you're also kicking people.

jokes.text: None of you will live with me in my hand-shaped mansion. Except you. You can live in the thumb.
jokes.setup: Joey's hand twin rejection
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fantasy mansion based on hands
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey absurdity - architectural fantasy based on body parts with specific thumb assignment

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A hand twin! Do you know how rare that is? We could do everything together! We could be hand models, we could open a hand modeling agency, we could have a mansion with a hand-shaped pool, and a hand-shaped hot tub, and little hand-shaped guest houses!

jokes.text: He said he thought I was funny.
jokes.setup: Ross asking what hotel person said about permanent marker
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's priorities even in crisis

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What did the hotel person say about the permanent marker?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: The only takeaway was a personal compliment.

jokes.text: Ross, I am a human doodle!
jokes.setup: Rachel's distress about drawn-on face
jokes.explanation: Perfect phrase that captures the absurdity with great economy and imagery

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Oh my God! There's a face drawn all over my face!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, yeah. We were trying to cheer you up.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: By drawing a face on my face?!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's the smiley face. You know, to make you smile.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, it's not working!

jokes.text: You dirty lurker!
jokes.setup: Phoebe's confrontation with old lady slot machine player
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Phoebe's gambling storyline with moral outrage

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know, it's not fair how you're treating her like a criminal just because she's winning.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She's been playing the same machine for six hours straight.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So? Maybe she's just really committed to it. That doesn't make her a bad person.

jokes.text: This hand is your hand, This hand is my hand, Oh, wait, that's your hand, No, wait, it's my hand
jokes.setup: Joey's song for hand twin act
jokes.punchline: Parody song getting confused about which hand is which
jokes.explanation: Good parody showing the inherent confusion in Joey's hand twin concept

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: 🎵 You're my hand twin, my hand twin, we look so much alike. You're my hand twin, my hand twin, I like you, I really like you. But which one are you? Are you the left or right? I can never tell, I can never tell, you're driving me crazy tonight! 🎵

jokes.text: You roll another hard eight... and we get married here tonight. Shut up!
jokes.setup: High-stakes gambling moment
jokes.punchline: Marriage proposal based on dice roll
jokes.explanation: Major plot moment with perfect Vegas spontaneity and character-appropriate proposal

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm going to roll the dice. If I get a seven, I'm proposing to Monica.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What?! Chandler, you can't base a marriage proposal on a dice roll!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why not? It's worked for everything else in my life.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, it hasn't!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, you're right. But I'm doing it anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Here goes... *rolls dice* Seven!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

jokes.text: What time? Maybe we can share a cab.
jokes.setup: Phoebe threatening to follow the lurker everywhere
jokes.punchline: Old lady's practical response about sharing transportation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of dramatic threat with practical elderly person logic

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm going to follow you everywhere you go.

dialogue.speaker: Old Lady
dialogue.line: Well, you could just take the bus with me.

jokes.text: Sells drugs to kids.
jokes.setup: Security asking about slot machine dispute
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Phoebe will say anything to win the argument

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: He stole my lucky harmonica in 1987!

jokes.text: My name is Regina Falangie. I'm a businesswoman in town on business.
jokes.setup: Phoebe getting kicked out, needing new identity
jokes.punchline: Fake name and redundant business description
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe fake identity with her characteristic redundant logic

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I need a fake name and a new identity.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'll be Regina Phalange, and I'm a... licensed masseuse and massage therapist.

jokes.text: If you leave now, I will chop off my hand and give it to you!
jokes.setup: Dealer's frustration with Joey's hand twin pitch
jokes.punchline: Extreme threat to get rid of Joey
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of dealer's desperation with darkly comic self-mutilation offer

dialogue.speaker: Dealer
dialogue.line: Joey, I appreciate the enthusiasm, but 'hand twin' is not a viable business model.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But my hands are perfect! Look at them!

dialogue.speaker: Dealer
dialogue.line: I don't care if your hands cure cancer. If you don't stop pitching this, I'm going to break both of them and feed them to my dog.

jokes.text: Well, hello, Mr. Rachel!
jokes.setup: Ross calling Monica 'Mrs. Ross'
jokes.explanation: Cute drunk logic moment showing Monica's confusion about wedding name conventions

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Mrs. Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm not Mrs. Ross. I'm Monica Geller.

tmdb_overview:
Chandler decides the only way to mend his friendship with Joey is to fly out to Vegas and visit him. Phoebe is determined not to be left out on another adventure, so everyone winds up going. Trouble is in the water, however, when Chandler finds out that Monica had lunch with Richard. Meanwhile, Ross and Rachel try to embarrass each other after an awkward encounter.

Chandler and Monica reconcile and hastily decide to get married. Ross and Rachel get drunk and roam the casino. Phoebe deals with a 'lurker' on the slot machines.
insight: Vegas chaos escalates to a 73-point climax fueled by character comedy and deadpan timing.
